purpose: A horizontal container for page-level actions and titles. Used as a header, footer, or subheader anchor for a page or dialog. Sits at the top or bottom of a container, with left/middle/right content regions.
whenToUse: As the header of a Dialog or Popover (contains title + close/accept buttons)
As the footer of a Dialog or Page (contains primary + secondary action buttons)
As a subheader inside a page below the main title
When you need three-region horizontal alignment (left / center / right)
whenNotToUse: Inside a Toolbar — Toolbar is a container of items, Bar is a page-level region
For a floating action bar — use OverflowToolbar with responsive behavior instead
As a container of general content — use Panel or Card
doRules: Use design=Header for a top bar, design=Footer for a bottom bar, design=Subheader for an intermediate bar
Place the primary action right-aligned in a Footer bar
Center the title in the contentMiddle slot for dialog headers
Use ToolbarSpacer inside a slot to push content to the edge
dontRules: Do not use a Bar for arbitrary layout — it is semantic (header/footer/subheader)
Do not stack multiple Bars in the same region — restructure the page instead
Do not put non-action content in a footer Bar

layoutGuidance.placement: Top or bottom of parent container (Dialog, Page)
layoutGuidance.sizing: Full width of parent. Height: 44px compact, 56px cozy
layoutGuidance.alignment: contentLeft: flush left · contentMiddle: centered · contentRight: flush right

contentGuidance.labelLength: Titles up to 40 characters. Truncate with ellipsis if longer.
contentGuidance.contentRules: Actions as Buttons, labels as Titles, no arbitrary text

responsiveBehavior.XL: Full width, all three regions visible
responsiveBehavior.S: Middle region may collapse; primary action stays right

requirements: Bar has aria-role of banner (header) or contentinfo (footer)
Title text has 4.5:1 contrast against bar background
Focus order: left content → middle → right actions
